steps.title: Identify the gratifying outcome
steps.content: The key signal is that Gogol finds it “gratifying” that his efforts “are to have some practical end.” The word “practical” is decisive—he's satisfied that his work will achieve something real and useful, which matters after years of schooling and unbuilt projects.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”End” means the purpose or goal that his efforts will achieve. “Practical end” translates directly to “practical purpose,” which fits perfectly: Gogol is gratified that his contributions will have a real, useful purpose.

The other three (limit, delay, decision) either don't make sense in context or contradict the gratifying tone.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
